name: Gamepads
shortDescription: Gamepad/controller support: buttons, sticks, triggers, vibration. Mapper behaviors for 2D/3D.

eventsBasedBehaviors.description: Control a platformer character with a gamepad.
eventsBasedBehaviors.name: PlatformerGamepadMapper

eventsBasedBehaviors.description: Control a 3D physics character with a gamepad.
eventsBasedBehaviors.name: Platformer3DGamepadMapper

eventsBasedBehaviors.description: Control a 3D physics character with a gamepad.
eventsBasedBehaviors.name: Shooter3DGamepadMapper

eventsBasedBehaviors.description: Control camera rotations with a gamepad.
eventsBasedBehaviors.name: FirstPersonGamepadMapper

eventsFunctions.name: HorizontalRotationSpeedMax
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the maximum horizontal rotation speed of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: SetHorizontalRotationSpeedMax
eventsFunctions.description: Change the maximum horizontal rotation speed of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: HorizontalRotationAcceleration
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the horizontal rotation acceleration of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: SetHorizontalRotationAcceleration
eventsFunctions.description: Change the horizontal rotation acceleration of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: HorizontalRotationDeceleration
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the horizontal rotation deceleration of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: SetHorizontalRotationDeceleration
eventsFunctions.description: Change the horizontal rotation deceleration of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: VerticalRotationSpeedMax
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the maximum vertical rotation speed of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: SetVerticalRotationSpeedMax
eventsFunctions.description: Change the maximum vertical rotation speed of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: VerticalRotationAcceleration
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the vertical rotation acceleration of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: SetVerticalRotationAcceleration
eventsFunctions.description: Change the vertical rotation acceleration of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: VerticalRotationDeceleration
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the vertical rotation deceleration of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: SetVerticalRotationDeceleration
eventsFunctions.description: Change the vertical rotation deceleration of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: VerticalAngleMin
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the minimum vertical camera angle of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: SetVerticalAngleMin
eventsFunctions.description: Change the minimum vertical camera angle of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: VerticalAngleMax
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the maximum vertical camera angle of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: SetVerticalAngleMax
eventsFunctions.description: Change the maximum vertical camera angle of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: OffsetZ
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the z position offset of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: SetOffsetZ
eventsFunctions.description: Change the z position offset of the object.

eventsBasedBehaviors.description: Control a 3D physics car with a gamepad.
eventsBasedBehaviors.name: PhysicsCar3DGamepadMapper

eventsBasedBehaviors.description: Control a top-down character with a gamepad.
eventsBasedBehaviors.name: TopDownGamepadMapper

eventsFunctions.name: TriggerPressure
eventsFunctions.description: Get the value of the pressure on a gamepad trigger.

eventsFunctions.name: StickForce
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the force of gamepad stick (from 0 to 1).

eventsFunctions.name: StickAngle
eventsFunctions.description:
Return the angle of a gamepad stick.
If the deadzone value is high, the angle value is rounded to main axes, left, left, up, down.
An zero deadzone value give a total freedom on the angle value.

eventsFunctions.name: StickForceX
eventsFunctions.description: Return the gamepad stick force on X axis (from -1 at the left to 1 at the right).

eventsFunctions.name: StickForceY
eventsFunctions.description: Return the gamepad stick force on Y axis (from -1 at the top to 1 at the bottom).

eventsFunctions.name: C_Button_released
eventsFunctions.description:
Test if a button is released on a gamepad. Buttons can be:
* Xbox: "A", "B", "X", "Y", "LB", "RB", "LT", "RT", "BACK", "START",
* PS4: "CROSS", "SQUARE", "CIRCLE", "TRIANGLE", "L1", "L2", "R1", "R2", "SHARE", "OPTIONS", "PS_BUTTON", "CLICK_TOUCHPAD",
* Other: "UP", "DOWN", "LEFT", "RIGHT", "CLICK_STICK_LEFT", "CLICK_STICK_RIGHT".

eventsFunctions.name: IsButtonJustPressed
eventsFunctions.description:
Check if a button was just pressed on a gamepad. Buttons can be:
* Xbox: "A", "B", "X", "Y", "LB", "RB", "LT", "RT", "BACK", "START",
* PS4: "CROSS", "SQUARE", "CIRCLE", "TRIANGLE", "L1", "L2", "R1", "R2", "SHARE", "OPTIONS", "PS_BUTTON", "CLICK_TOUCHPAD",
* Other: "UP", "DOWN", "LEFT", "RIGHT", "CLICK_STICK_LEFT", "CLICK_STICK_RIGHT".

eventsFunctions.name: LastButtonID
eventsFunctions.description: Return the index of the last pressed button of a gamepad.

eventsFunctions.name: C_Any_Button_pressed
eventsFunctions.description: Check if any button is pressed on a gamepad.

eventsFunctions.name: LastButtonString
eventsFunctions.description:
Return the last button pressed. 
Buttons for Xbox and PS4 can be:
* Xbox: "A", "B", "X", "Y", "LB", "RB", "LT", "RT", "BACK", "START",
* PS4: "CROSS", "SQUARE", "CIRCLE", "TRIANGLE", "L1", "L2", "R1", "R2", "SHARE", "OPTIONS", "PS_BUTTON", "CLICK_TOUCHPAD",
* Both: "UP", "DOWN", "LEFT", "RIGHT", "CLICK_STICK_LEFT", "CLICK_STICK_RIGHT".

eventsFunctions.name: GamepadCount
eventsFunctions.description: Return the number of gamepads.

eventsFunctions.name: C_Button_pressed
eventsFunctions.description:
Check if a button is pressed on a gamepad. 
Buttons can be:
* Xbox: "A", "B", "X", "Y", "LB", "RB", "LT", "RT", "BACK", "START",
* PS4: "CROSS", "SQUARE", "CIRCLE", "TRIANGLE", "L1", "L2", "R1", "R2", "SHARE", "OPTIONS", "PS_BUTTON", "CLICK_TOUCHPAD",
* Other: "UP", "DOWN", "LEFT", "RIGHT", "CLICK_STICK_LEFT", "CLICK_STICK_RIGHT".

eventsFunctions.name: Deadzone
eventsFunctions.description: Return the value of the deadzone applied to a gamepad sticks, between 0 and 1.

eventsFunctions.name: A_Set_deadzone
eventsFunctions.description: Set the deadzone for sticks of the gamepad. The deadzone is an area for which movement on sticks won't be taken into account (instead, the stick will be considered as not moved). Deadzone is between 0 and 1, and is by default 0.2.

eventsFunctions.name: C_Axis_pushed
eventsFunctions.description: Check if a stick of a gamepad is pushed in a given direction.

eventsFunctions.name: ConnectedGamepadsCount
eventsFunctions.description: Return the number of connected gamepads.

eventsFunctions.name: GamepadType
eventsFunctions.description: Return a string containing informations about the specified gamepad.

eventsFunctions.name: C_Controller_type
eventsFunctions.description: Check if the specified gamepad has the specified information in its description. Useful to know if the gamepad is a Xbox or PS4 controller.

eventsFunctions.name: C_Controller_X_is_connected
eventsFunctions.description: Check if a gamepad is connected.

eventsFunctions.name: A_Vibrate_controller
eventsFunctions.description: Generate a vibration on the specified controller. Might only work if the game is running in a recent web browser.

eventsFunctions.name: A_Advanced_Vibration_Controller
eventsFunctions.description: Generate an advanced vibration on the specified controller. Incompatible with Firefox.

eventsFunctions.name: A_Change_Vibration_Magnitude
eventsFunctions.description: Change a vibration on the specified controller. Incompatible with Firefox.

eventsFunctions.name: C_any_button_released
eventsFunctions.description: Check if any button is released on a gamepad.

eventsFunctions.name: WeakVibrationMagnitude
eventsFunctions.description: Return the strength of the weak vibration motor on the gamepad of a player.

eventsFunctions.name: StrongVibrationMagnitude
eventsFunctions.description: Return the strength of the strong vibration motor on the gamepad of a player.

description:
Add support for gamepads (or other physical controllers).

It gives access to:
- button presses,
- axis positions and force,
- trigger pressure,
- configurable deadzone,
- vibration,
- automatic "gamepad mapper" behaviors for 2D and 3D movement behaviors (platformer characters, top-down movement, 3D character etc...). Add one of these to the object with the movement behavior (i.e: the player most of the time) and the behavior will then be controlled automatically by the gamepad (it works by reading the gamepad state and simulating controls). No need for additional events to make it work once the behaviors are set up.

The Bomberman-like example handles 4 players with gamepads ([open the project online](https://editor.gdevelop.io/?project=example://goose-bomberman)).
